subject: June 15 close — agenda + items I need
snippet: Jacob — ahead of Monday's check-in, a few things to pull together...
body:
Jacob,

Ahead of Monday's check-in (the 15th, usual 10 AM), a few things to pull together: May bank rec from Janet, the Saxco invoice that hit on 5/28, and the barrel CapEx entries so we book them right.

Also want 15 minutes on the aging-inventory line. With 140+ barrels in the rickhouse the carrying cost is starting to matter for the credit line conversation with First Business.

Derek
Holmgren & Associates

subject: Re: June 15 close — agenda + items I need
snippet: Works. Janet's cc'd — she'll have the May rec done Thursday...
body:
Works. Janet's cc'd — she'll have the May rec done Thursday. Saxco invoice is in the QuickBooks inbox already, $4,180 for the 750ml flint order.

On aging inventory: agreed. I'd rather have that conversation with real numbers than hand-wave it. Bring whatever schedule format the bank wants to see.

Jacob

subject: Re: June 15 close — agenda + items I need
snippet: Perfect. One more: the Q2 estimated payment is due 6/15 as well...
body:
Perfect. One more: the Q2 estimated payment is due 6/15 as well — I'll have the voucher amounts ready Monday morning so you can authorize both at once.

See you at 10.

Derek

subject: Submission received: Monthly Report of Operations (May 2026) — DSP-WI-21044
snippet: Your TTB F 5110.40 Monthly Report of Processing Operations for May 2026 has been received...
body:
This message confirms that your TTB F 5110.40 Monthly Report of Processing Operations for the period May 2026 has been received for permit DSP-WI-21044, Heartland Spirits LLC.

Submission ID: PONL-2026-0602-88419
Status: Accepted

Do not reply to this message. For questions, contact the National Revenue Center.

subject: June order + a favor
snippet: Jacob — usual June order, plus can you hold me two cases of the cask strength...
body:
Jacob,

Usual June order: 3 cases Bourbon 4-Year, 2 cases Rye, 1 case Driftless Gin. Same delivery window as always, Tuesday morning before prep.

The favor: can you hold me two cases of the Cask Strength 2025 when the next allocation opens? We're building a Wisconsin-only spirits page for fall and I want it anchored on your bottle.

Marco

subject: Re: June order + a favor
snippet: Done on both. Order confirmed for Tuesday the 16th...
body:
Done on both. Order confirmed for Tuesday the 16th, Tyler will have it on the truck by 9.

Two cases of HS-BRBCS-750 are yours — allocation opens to the bottle club July 1, so you're getting them before the list even sees it. Don't tell Star Liquor.

A Wisconsin-only page anchored on our bottle is the nicest thing anyone's said to me this week.

Jacob

subject: Re: June order + a favor
snippet: You're a gentleman. Invoice to the usual address...
body:
You're a gentleman. Invoice to the usual address.

One more thing — October 17 is your tenth, no? We should talk about Osteria doing a course pairing for the anniversary party. On the house. You've earned it.

Marco

subject: Re: June order + a favor
snippet: Yes — ten years October 17. A pairing course would make the night...
body:
Yes — ten years October 17. A pairing course would make the night. Alana's running point on the party; she'll set up a call with you in July to sort logistics.

Invoice goes out today.

Jacob

subject: Reorder — bourbon moving fast
snippet: Jacob — the 4-Year is down to six bottles on the shelf...
body:
Jacob,

The 4-Year is down to six bottles on the shelf and the vodka's close behind. Can we get 4 cases HS-BRB-750 and 2 cases HS-VOD-750 this week?

Also customers keep asking when the next gin botanical rotation drops. What do I tell them?

Tim
Star Liquor, Williamson St

subject: Re: Reorder — bourbon moving fast
snippet: On the truck Thursday. Summer gin bottles late June...
body:
On the truck Thursday — 4 cases bourbon, 2 vodka.

Summer gin rotation bottles the last week of June: elderflower, lemon balm, and a little spruce tip from a grower up in Bayfield. Tell them it's worth the wait, because it is.

Jacob

subject: 2026 crop allocation — short-grain for Heartland
snippet: Hi Jacob — locking in fall allocations now. You asked about 2,400 lbs of short-grain...
body:
Hi Jacob,

Locking in fall allocations now. You asked about 2,400 lbs of short-grain for the rice spirit project — we can do that at $0.92/lb, milled to your spec, delivery split September and November.

The September lot would come off the same paddies as last year's test batch, which I know mattered to you for consistency.

Anna
Driftless Rice Collective

subject: Re: 2026 crop allocation — short-grain for Heartland
snippet: Same paddies is exactly what I wanted to hear. Book the 2,400 lbs...
body:
Same paddies is exactly what I wanted to hear — the koji took to that starch profile better than anything else we ran. Book the 2,400 lbs, split delivery as proposed.

Cc'ing Tyler so receiving knows what's coming in September. I'm presenting the rice-spirit work at ACSA Midwest in Milwaukee this November and your co-op is getting named from the podium, for what that's worth.

Jacob

subject: Re: 2026 crop allocation — short-grain for Heartland
snippet: Booked. Contract attached for signature...
body:
Booked. Contract attached for signature — net 30, half on each delivery.

And it's worth a lot. Small co-op, big podium. Good luck in Milwaukee.

Anna

subject: Fresh koji batch ready — pickup or drop-off?
snippet: Jacob — the white koji you wanted for the June mash trials is ready...
body:
Jacob,

The white koji (kawachii) you wanted for the June mash trials is ready — 20 lbs, spore activity looks excellent. Citric production should be right where you need it for the wash pH.

Pickup at the shop or should I drop it on Williamson St Wednesday?

Sam

subject: Re: Fresh koji batch ready — pickup or drop-off?
snippet: Drop Wednesday works — Tyler's on the floor from 6...
body:
Drop Wednesday works — Tyler's on the floor from 6, just ring the dock bell.

If the citric lands where you say, this is the batch that goes in the ACSA deck. I'll send you the gravity curves once we're a week into ferment.

Jacob

subject: Quote Q-18834 — 25x 53gal new charred oak, #3 char
snippet: Per your request: 25 new 53-gallon charred oak barrels, #3 char...
body:
Per your request: 25 new 53-gallon American oak barrels, #3 char, at $295/barrel, FOB Lincoln NE. Freight to Madison estimated $640. Lead time 6 weeks from PO.

Quote valid 30 days. Reference Q-18834 on your PO.

Midwest Barrel Co. Sales

subject: Re: Quote Q-18834 — 25x 53gal new charred oak, #3 char
snippet: Accepting Q-18834 for 20 barrels rather than 25...
body:
Accepting Q-18834 at 20 barrels rather than 25 — cash flow says twenty this quarter. PO to follow from our bookkeeper today.

Delivery to 1402 Williamson St, Madison, dock hours Mon–Wed 6 AM–2 PM.

Jacob Graves
Heartland Spirits LLC

subject: FS 572 fall scheduling — confirm by June 20
snippet: Hi Jacob — confirming your fall appointment for FS 572 Fermented Food & Beverage Science...
body:
Hi Jacob,

Confirming your fall adjunct appointment for FS 572 Fermented Food & Beverage Science: Tue/Thu 2:00–3:15 PM, Babcock Hall 121, first lecture September 8. Enrollment cap stays at 36; we already have 31 registered.

Please confirm by June 20 so I can finalize the timetable, and send any syllabus updates to me rather than the registrar this year.

Karen Voss
Department of Food Science

subject: Re: FS 572 fall scheduling — confirm by June 20
snippet: Confirmed — Tue/Thu 2:00 works. Syllabus updates coming...
body:
Confirmed — Tue/Thu 2:00–3:15 works, Babcock 121 is home at this point.

Syllabus updates by mid-July: I'm adding a two-week koji/rice fermentation module and retiring the old cheese-adjacent unit Dr. Liang and I discussed. Office hours stay Thursday 3:30–5.

31 registered in June is a good sign. Thanks Karen.

Jacob

subject: Re: FS 572 fall scheduling — confirm by June 20
snippet: Marked confirmed. One admin note on the appointment letter...
body:
Marked confirmed, thank you.

One admin note: your appointment letter will route through the new HR portal this year — watch for an email from hr@ohr.wisc.edu in July and don't let it sit, the signing window is short.

Karen

subject: ACSA abstract — comments back
snippet: Jacob — read the draft on the flight back from the IFT board meeting. It's good...
body:
Jacob,

Read your ACSA abstract draft on the flight back from the IFT board meeting. It's good. Two suggestions:

1. Lead with the koji saccharification data, not the cultural framing — the Milwaukee crowd will be distillers first.
2. The Midwest-grown rice angle is your differentiator. Quantify it: terroir claims need numbers or they're marketing.

Happy to read one more pass before the June 30 submission deadline.

Patricia

subject: Re: ACSA abstract — comments back
snippet: Both points taken. Reordering now — data first, story second...
body:
Both points taken. Reordering now: saccharification curves up top, the rice sourcing gets a yields-per-acre and starch-content table instead of adjectives.

Revision to you by June 15. And thank you — six years out of grad school and you're still grading my work, which is either touching or concerning.

Jacob

subject: Appointment Reminder - UW Health
snippet: This is a reminder of your upcoming appointment. Please log in to MyChart for details...
body:
This is a reminder of an upcoming appointment at UW Health.

Please log in to MyChart to view details, complete eCheck-in, or reschedule. Arrive 15 minutes early if this is your first visit to this clinic location.

Do not reply to this message.

snippet: Your upper endoscopy with Dr. Amy Larson is confirmed for Friday, October 30, 2026 at 8:00 AM...
body:
Your upper endoscopy with Dr. Amy Larson is confirmed for Friday, October 30, 2026 at 8:00 AM, Quassia Medical Endoscopy Center.

Prep instructions: nothing by mouth after midnight. You will need a driver for discharge. Pre-procedure questionnaire will be sent 2 weeks prior.

To reschedule call (608) 258-0267.

subject: Your cleaning is scheduled — Tuesday, October 13
snippet: Hi Jacob, this confirms your dental cleaning on Tuesday, October 13, 2026 at 7:30 AM...
body:
Hi Jacob,

This confirms your dental cleaning on Tuesday, October 13, 2026 at 7:30 AM with Dr. Reyes.

Reply C to confirm or call the office to reschedule. We'll send another reminder one week before.

Monroe Street Dental

subject: Thursday's meeting — can you bring the banchan again?
snippet: Hey Jacob — group meets Thursday the 11th, 7 PM at my place as usual...
body:
Hey Jacob,

Group meets Thursday the 11th, 7 PM at my place as usual. We have two newcomers this month — both adopted from Seoul in the late 80s, so your story will land.

And yes, this is also me asking if you'd bring the banchan again. The kkakdugi from April is still being talked about.

Sarah

subject: Re: Thursday's meeting — can you bring the banchan again?
snippet: I'll be there at 7, banchan in hand. Made a fresh kkakdugi batch Sunday...
body:
I'll be there at 7, banchan in hand. Made a fresh kkakdugi batch Sunday, plus some japchae since we have newcomers and japchae is the universal icebreaker.

Glad they found the group. Happy to talk after if either of them wants to.

Jacob

subject: Re: Thursday's meeting — can you bring the banchan again?
snippet: You're the best. Also — hold Oct 8 for the fall regional meeting...
body:
You're the best.

Also — hold October 8 on your calendar. The fall regional KAAN meeting is confirmed for Madison this year and I want you on the panel again. Details next month.

Sarah

subject: Weekend coverage + two private tasting requests
snippet: Two things: Emma can't do Saturday, and we got two private tasting inquiries...
body:
Two things:

1. Emma can't do Saturday — wedding. Dev says he'll cover the full shift if we're OK with him leaving at 9 instead of close Sunday.

2. Two private tasting inquiries this week: a 12-person corporate group for June 26 and a bachelorette for July 11. Corporate yes, bachelorette I want to vet — they asked if they could 'bring their own mixers' which is a sentence that worries me.

Alana

subject: Re: Weekend coverage + two private tasting requests
snippet: Dev's trade is fine. Corporate yes; trust your gut on the other one...
body:
Dev's trade is fine — Sunday nights are dead by 9 anyway.

Corporate group: yes, standard private rate. Bachelorette: trust your gut. Our private tastings are educational pours, not a party room — if they balk at that framing, they're not our booking.

Jacob

Dear distillery owner,

Sophisticated investors are earning double-digit returns on rare cask whisky. As an industry insider, you qualify for our exclusive partner tier. Reply now to reserve your allocation before this window closes.

Premium Cask Partners

subject: Monday numbers — bourbon mash 26-14
snippet: Mash 26-14 in the cooker by 6:40. OG 1.062, corn from the new Kelby lot...
body:
Mash 26-14 in the cooker by 6:40. OG 1.062, corn from the new Kelby lot — conversion looks normal, iodine clean at 45 min.

FV-3 is acting up again though. Glycol valve is slow to respond; she crept to 89.5 overnight before the controller caught it. We should get Hartwig out before we trust her with the rice wash next month.

Tyler

subject: Re: Monday numbers — bourbon mash 26-14
snippet: Good catch. Call Hartwig today — I'd rather eat a service call than lose a rice wash...
body:
Good catch. Call Hartwig today — I'd rather eat a service call than lose a rice wash to a lazy valve. If they can't come before the 22nd, we move the rice trial to FV-1 and shuffle the gin around it.

1.062 on the new Kelby corn is right in band. Log it against the lot number so we have the comparison when pricing talks come up in August.

Jacob

subject: Featuring the Rye in our summer Old Fashioned flight
snippet: Jacob — we're building a Wisconsin rye flight for summer and want Heartland Rye in the lineup...
body:
Jacob,

We're building a Wisconsin rye flight for the summer menu and want Heartland Rye in the lineup. Probably means doubling our usual order — call it 4 cases/month June through August.

Can you hold pricing at the current wholesale rate for the season if we commit to the volume?

Katie
Beverage Director, The Old Fashioned

subject: Re: Featuring the Rye in our summer Old Fashioned flight
snippet: Yes on volume, yes on holding price through August...
body:
Yes on volume, yes on holding price through August with the 3-month commit. Grain costs are climbing so I can't promise past Labor Day, but summer's locked.

A Wisconsin rye flight at the Old Fashioned is exactly where that whiskey belongs. First doubled order ships next week.

Jacob
